Skip to contents
statuAAR 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference

Returns the list of stature estimates from getStature() as data.frame.

Source: R/functions.R
getStatureDataframe.Rd

Returns the data frames of the GetStature list with an additional column for the formula used.

Usage

getStatureDataframe(StatureList)

Arguments

StatureList,

the return of getStature().

Value

A data.frame with eight columns:

  • formula: short name of the formula, e.g. bb65,

  • ind: individual identifyer (rownames),

  • sex: as provided for calculation: m, f, indet.

  • stature: estimated on the provided sex and bone measures,

  • bone measure name(s): bones used for calculation,

  • female stature: columns with alternative stature for three sex classes,

  • male stature,

  • indet. stature and

  • n_measures: number of bone measures included: e.g. 2 Fem2 (left, right) + 1 Tib1

Author

Christoph Rinne crinne@ufg.uni-kiel.de

Examples

# Get the bone measures needed for the functions.
getStatureDataframe(getStature(c('bb65', 'tg01'), dl.trotter.gleser))
#>     formula   id sex stature       bone if_female if_male if_indet n_measures
#> 1      bb65  1_1   m    1551 Hum1, Fem1      1557    1551     1554          2
#> 2      bb65 1_10   m    1617 Hum1, Fem1      1609    1617     1613          2
#> 3      bb65 1_11   m    1629 Hum1, Fem1      1618    1629     1623          2
#> 4      bb65 1_12   m    1637 Hum1, Fem1      1625    1637     1631          2
#> 5      bb65 1_13   m    1645 Hum1, Fem1      1631    1645     1638          2
#> 6      bb65 1_14   m    1652 Hum1, Fem1      1637    1652     1644          2
#> 7      bb65 1_15   m    1660 Hum1, Fem1      1643    1660     1652          2
#> 8      bb65 1_16   m    1669 Hum1, Fem1      1650    1669     1659          2
#> 9      bb65 1_17   m    1676 Hum1, Fem1      1656    1676     1666          2
#> 10     bb65 1_18   m    1684 Hum1, Fem1      1661    1684     1673          2
#> 11     bb65 1_19   m    1691 Hum1, Fem1      1667    1691     1679          2
#> 12     bb65  1_2   m    1558 Hum1, Fem1      1562    1558     1560          2
#> 13     bb65 1_20   m    1700 Hum1, Fem1      1675    1700     1688          2
#> 14     bb65 1_21   m    1708 Hum1, Fem1      1681    1708     1694          2
#> 15     bb65 1_22   m    1715 Hum1, Fem1      1686    1715     1701          2
#> 16     bb65 1_23   m    1723 Hum1, Fem1      1692    1723     1707          2
#> 17     bb65 1_24   m    1730 Hum1, Fem1      1698    1730     1714          2

#> 21     bb65 1_28   m    1761 Hum1, Fem1      1723    1761     1742          2
